name: hermes-block-and-allow
doc: Hermes wire-shape parity. Nous Research's hermes-agent pipes a `pre_tool_call` payload (same INPUT shape as Claude Code) and reads JSON back; a block is `{decision:"block",reason}` (respond.ts:64 → adapters/hermes/toHermesResponse), an allow prints EMPTY stdout (the `hook` command emits the allow-path assembly, which for a plain read is "" — NOT `{}`; the adapter's own guard() would print `{}` but that path is unused by `hook`). Same guard/gate chain as every other harness — only the envelope differs. Real output verified 2026-07-06 against `bun src/cli/bin.ts hook hermes core`.

steps.doc: Destructive git via the hermes `terminal` tool → GIT_BLOCKED deny, rendered as `{decision:"block"}`. Asserts the block decision AND that the tool_name is irrelevant to the git gate (evaluate keys off ctx.command, not the tool name).

150-line `write_file` (hermes path+content) > 100 → SOLID file-size block, proving the file-size gate runs on the hermes edition too (not just Claude). Assert the [BLOCKED] title survives into `reason`.
A plain read (`ls`) via `terminal` → allowed. The `hook` allow-path prints EMPTY stdout on hermes (verified) — the parity check that an allow is silent, NOT `{}`.
